name: smbmap
title: smbmap

commands.name: smbmap

arguments.description: Define a file name pattern (regex) that auto downloads a file on a match (requires -r),
arguments.raw: -A  Define a file name pattern (regex) that auto downloads a file on a match (requires -r),

arguments.description: Just report if the user is an admin
arguments.raw: --admin  Just report if the user is an admin

arguments.description: Output to a CSV file, ex --csv shares.csv
arguments.raw: --csv  Output to a CSV file, ex --csv shares.csv

arguments.description: Domain name (default WORKGROUP)
arguments.raw: -d  Domain name (default WORKGROUP)

arguments.description: Delete a remote file, ex. 'C$\temp\msf.exe'
arguments.raw: --delete  Delete a remote file, ex. 'C$\temp\msf.exe'

arguments.description: Traverse a directory tree to a specific depth. Default is 1 (root node).
arguments.raw: --depth  Traverse a directory tree to a specific depth. Default is 1 (root node).

arguments.description: List only directories, omit files
arguments.raw: --dir-only  List only directories, omit files

arguments.description: Download a file from the remote system, ex.'C$\temp\passwords.txt'
arguments.raw: --download  Download a file from the remote system, ex.'C$\temp\passwords.txt'

arguments.description: Output to a file in a grep friendly format, used with -r (otherwise it outputs noth-
arguments.raw: -g  Output to a file in a grep friendly format, used with -r (otherwise it outputs noth-

arguments.description: File containing a list of hosts
arguments.raw: --host-file  File containing a list of hosts

arguments.description: Use Kerberos authentication
arguments.raw: -k  Use Kerberos authentication

arguments.description: Use Kerberos authentication
arguments.raw: --kerberos  Use Kerberos authentication

arguments.description: List all drives on the specified host, requires ADMIN rights.
arguments.raw: -L  List all drives on the specified host, requires ADMIN rights.

arguments.description: Set the execution method, wmi or psexec, default wmi
arguments.raw: --mode  Set the execution method, wmi or psexec, default wmi

arguments.description: Removes the banner from the top of the output
arguments.raw: --no-banner  Removes the banner from the top of the output

arguments.description: Removes the color from output
arguments.raw: --no-color  Removes the color from output

arguments.description: Use CCache file (export KRB5CCNAME='~/current.ccache')
arguments.raw: --no-pass  Use CCache file (export KRB5CCNAME='~/current.ccache')

arguments.description: Removes the "Working on it" message
arguments.raw: --no-update  Removes the "Working on it" message

arguments.description: Skip check to see if drive grants WRITE access
arguments.raw: --no-write-check  Skip check to see if drive grants WRITE access

arguments.description: SMB port (default 445)
arguments.raw: -P  SMB port (default 445)

arguments.description: Prompt for a password
arguments.raw: --prompt  Prompt for a password

arguments.description: Quiet verbose output. Only shows shares you have READ or WRITE on, and suppresses file
arguments.raw: -q  Quiet verbose output. Only shows shares you have READ or WRITE on, and suppresses file

arguments.description: Recursively list dirs and files (no shareath
arguments.raw: -r  Recursively list dirs and files (no shareath

arguments.description: Specify a share (default C$), ex 'C$'
arguments.raw: -s  Specify a share (default C$), ex 'C$'

arguments.description: Specify drive/path to search (used with -F, default C:\Users), ex 'D:\HR\'
arguments.raw: --search-path  Specify drive/path to search (used with -F, default C:\Users), ex 'D:\HR\'

arguments.description: Specifcy a timeout (in seconds) before the file search job gets killed. Default is 300
arguments.raw: --search-timeout  Specifcy a timeout (in seconds) before the file search job gets killed. Default is 300

arguments.description: Check if host has SMB signing disabled, enabled, or required
arguments.raw: --signing  Check if host has SMB signing disabled, enabled, or required

arguments.description: Skip delete file confirmation prompt
arguments.raw: --skip  Skip delete file confirmation prompt

arguments.description: Set port scan socket timeout. Default is .5 seconds
arguments.raw: --timeout  Set port scan socket timeout. Default is .5 seconds

arguments.description: Return the OS version of the remote host
arguments.raw: -v  Return the OS version of the remote host

arguments.description: Return the OS version of the remote host
arguments.raw: --version  Return the OS version of the remote host

arguments.description: Execute a command ex. 'ipconfig /all'
arguments.raw: -x  Execute a command ex. 'ipconfig /all'

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# smbmap -h
 usage: smbmap [-h] (-H HOST | --host-file FILE) [-u USERNAME] [-p PASSWORD |
             --prompt] [-k] [--no-pass] [--dc-ip IP or Host] [-s SHARE]
             [-d DOMAIN] [-P PORT] [-v] [--signing] [--admin] [--no-banner]
             [--no-color] [--no-update] [--timeout SCAN_TIMEOUT] [-x COMMAND]
             [--mode CMDMODE] [-L | -r [PATH]] [-g FILE | --csv FILE]
             [--dir-only] [--no-write-check] [-q] [--depth DEPTH]
             [--exclude SHARE [SHARE ...]] [-A PATTERN] [-F PATTERN]
             [--search-path PATH] [--search-timeout TIMEOUT]
             [--download PATH] [--upload SRC DST] [--delete PATH TO FILE]
             [--skip]
 
   ________  ___      ___  _______   ___      ___       __         _______

 (:   \___/  \   \  //   |(. |_)  :) \   \  //   |    /    \      (. |__) :)
  \___  \    /\  \/.    ||:     \/   /\   \/.    |   /' /\  \     |:  ____/
   __/  \   |: \.        |(|  _  \  |: \.        |  //  __'  \    (|  /
  /" \   :) |.  \    /:  ||: |_)  :)|.  \    /:  | /   /  \   \  /|__/ \
 (_______/  |___|\__/|___|(_______/ |___|\__/|___|(___/    \___)(_______)
 -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
 SMBMap - Samba Share Enumerator v1.10.7 | Shawn Evans - ShawnDEvans@gmail.com
                    https://github.com/ShawnDEvans/smbmap
 
 options:
 -h, --help            show this help message and exit
 
 Main arguments:
 -H HOST               IP or FQDN
 --host-file FILE      File containing a list of hosts
 -u, --username USERNAME
                       Username, if omitted null session assumed
 -p, --password PASSWORD
                       Password or NTLM hash, format is LMHASH:NTHASH
 --prompt              Prompt for a password
 -s SHARE              Specify a share (default C$), ex 'C$'
 -d DOMAIN             Domain name (default WORKGROUP)
 -P PORT               SMB port (default 445)
 -v, --version         Return the OS version of the remote host
 --signing             Check if host has SMB signing disabled, enabled, or
                       required
 --admin               Just report if the user is an admin
 --no-banner           Removes the banner from the top of the output
 --no-color            Removes the color from output
 --no-update           Removes the "Working on it" message
 --timeout SCAN_TIMEOUT
                       Set port scan socket timeout. Default is .5 seconds
 
 Kerberos settings:
 -k, --kerberos        Use Kerberos authentication
 --no-pass             Use CCache file (export KRB5CCNAME='~/current.ccache')
 --dc-ip IP or Host    IP or FQDN of DC
 
 Command Execution:
 Options for executing commands on the specified host
 
 -x COMMAND            Execute a command ex. 'ipconfig /all'
 --mode CMDMODE        Set the execution method, wmi or psexec, default wmi
 
 Shard drive Search:
 Options for searching/enumerating the share of the specified host(s)
 
 -L                    List all drives on the specified host, requires ADMIN
                       rights.
 -r [PATH]             Recursively list dirs and files (no share\path lists
                       the root of ALL shares), ex. 'email/backup'
 -g FILE               Output to a file in a grep friendly format, used with
                       -r (otherwise it outputs nothing), ex -g grep_out.txt
 --csv FILE            Output to a CSV file, ex --csv shares.csv
 --dir-only            List only directories, ommit files.
 --no-write-check      Skip check to see if drive grants WRITE access.
 -q                    Quiet verbose output. Only shows shares you have READ
                       or WRITE on, and suppresses file listing when
                       performing a search (-A).
 --depth DEPTH         Traverse a directory tree to a specific depth. Default
                       is 1 (root node).
 --exclude SHARE [SHARE ...]
                       Exclude share(s) from searching and listing, ex.
                       --exclude ADMIN$ C$'
 -A PATTERN            Define a file name pattern (regex) that auto downloads
                       a file on a match (requires -r), not case sensitive,
                       ex '(web|global).(asax|config)'
 
 File Content Search:
 Options for searching the content of files (must run as root), kind of experimental
 
 -F PATTERN            File content search, -F '[Pp]assword' (requires admin
                       access to execute commands, and PowerShell on victim
                       host)
 --search-path PATH    Specify drive/path to search (used with -F, default
                       C:\Users), ex 'D:\HR\'
 --search-timeout TIMEOUT
                       Specifcy a timeout (in seconds) before the file search
                       job gets killed. Default is 300 seconds.
 
 Filesystem interaction:
 Options for interacting with the specified host's filesystem
 
 --download PATH       Download a file from the remote system,
                       ex.'C$\temp\passwords.txt'
 --upload SRC DST      Upload a file to the remote system ex.
                       '/tmp/payload.exe C$\temp\payload.exe'
 --delete PATH TO FILE
                       Delete a remote file, ex. 'C$\temp\msf.exe'
 --skip                Skip delete file confirmation prompt
 
 Examples:
 
 $ smbmap -u jsmith -p password1 -d workgroup -H 192.168.0.1
 $ smbmap -u jsmith -p 'aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee:da76f2c4c96028b7a6111aef4a50a94d' -H 172.16.0.20
 $ smbmap -u 'apadmin' -p 'asdf1234!' -d ACME -Hh 10.1.3.30 -x 'net group "Domain Admins" /domain'

examples.command:
smbmap -u victim -p s3cr3t -H 192.168.86.61
[+] Finding open SMB ports....
[+] User SMB session establishd on 192.168.86.61...
[+] IP: 192.168.86.61:445   Name: win7-x86.lan
    Disk                                                    Permissions
    ----                                                    -----------
    ADMIN$                                              NO ACCESS
    C$                                                  NO ACCESS
    IPC$                                                NO ACCESS
    Users                                               READ ONLY

usage: smbmap [-h] (-H HOST | --host-file FILE) [-u USERNAME] [-p PASSWORD |
              --prompt] [-k] [--no-pass] [--dc-ip IP or Host] [-s SHARE]
              [-d DOMAIN] [-P PORT] [-v] [--signing] [--admin] [--no-banner]
              [--no-color] [--no-update] [--timeout SCAN_TIMEOUT] [-x COMMAND]
              [--mode CMDMODE] [-L | -r [PATH]] [-g FILE | --csv FILE]
              [--dir-only] [--no-write-check] [-q] [--depth DEPTH]
              [--exclude SHARE [SHARE ...]] [-A PATTERN] [-F PATTERN]
              [--search-path PATH] [--search-timeout TIMEOUT]
              [--download PATH] [--upload SRC DST] [--delete PATH TO FILE]
              [--skip]

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
SMBMap - Samba Share Enumerator v1.10.7 | Shawn Evans - ShawnDEvans@gmail.com
                     https://github.com/ShawnDEvans/smbmap

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit

Main arguments:
  -H HOST               IP or FQDN
  --host-file FILE      File containing a list of hosts
  -u, --username USERNAME
                        Username, if omitted null session assumed
  -p, --password PASSWORD
                        Password or NTLM hash, format is LMHASH:NTHASH
  --prompt              Prompt for a password
  -s SHARE              Specify a share (default C$), ex 'C$'
  -d DOMAIN             Domain name (default WORKGROUP)
  -P PORT               SMB port (default 445)
  -v, --version         Return the OS version of the remote host
  --signing             Check if host has SMB signing disabled, enabled, or
                        required
  --admin               Just report if the user is an admin
  --no-banner           Removes the banner from the top of the output
  --no-color            Removes the color from output
  --no-update           Removes the "Working on it" message
  --timeout SCAN_TIMEOUT
                        Set port scan socket timeout. Default is .5 seconds

Kerberos settings:
  -k, --kerberos        Use Kerberos authentication
  --no-pass             Use CCache file (export KRB5CCNAME='~/current.ccache')
  --dc-ip IP or Host    IP or FQDN of DC

Command Execution:
  Options for executing commands on the specified host

  -x COMMAND            Execute a command ex. 'ipconfig /all'
  --mode CMDMODE        Set the execution method, wmi or psexec, default wmi

Shard drive Search:
  Options for searching/enumerating the share of the specified host(s)

  -L                    List all drives on the specified host, requires ADMIN
                        rights.
  -r [PATH]             Recursively list dirs and files (no share\path lists
                        the root of ALL shares), ex. 'email/backup'
  -g FILE               Output to a file in a grep friendly format, used with
                        -r (otherwise it outputs nothing), ex -g grep_out.txt
  --csv FILE            Output to a CSV file, ex --csv shares.csv
  --dir-only            List only directories, ommit files.
  --no-write-check      Skip check to see if drive grants WRITE access.
  -q                    Quiet verbose output. Only shows shares you have READ
                        or WRITE on, and suppresses file listing when
                        performing a search (-A).
  --depth DEPTH         Traverse a directory tree to a specific depth. Default
                        is 1 (root node).
  --exclude SHARE [SHARE ...]
                        Exclude share(s) from searching and listing, ex.
                        --exclude ADMIN$ C$'
  -A PATTERN            Define a file name pattern (regex) that auto downloads
                        a file on a match (requires -r), not case sensitive,
                        ex '(web|global).(asax|config)'

File Content Search:
  Options for searching the content of files (must run as root), kind of experimental

  -F PATTERN            File content search, -F '[Pp]assword' (requires admin
                        access to execute commands, and PowerShell on victim
                        host)
  --search-path PATH    Specify drive/path to search (used with -F, default
                        C:\Users), ex 'D:\HR\'
  --search-timeout TIMEOUT
                        Specifcy a timeout (in seconds) before the file search
                        job gets killed. Default is 300 seconds.

Filesystem interaction:
  Options for interacting with the specified host's filesystem

  --download PATH       Download a file from the remote system,
                        ex.'C$\temp\passwords.txt'
  --upload SRC DST      Upload a file to the remote system ex.
                        '/tmp/payload.exe C$\temp\payload.exe'
  --delete PATH TO FILE
                        Delete a remote file, ex. 'C$\temp\msf.exe'
  --skip                Skip delete file confirmation prompt

Examples:

$ smbmap -u jsmith -p password1 -d workgroup -H 192.168.0.1
$ smbmap -u jsmith -p 'aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee:da76f2c4c96028b7a6111aef4a50a94d' -H 172.16.0.20
$ smbmap -u 'apadmin' -p 'asdf1234!' -d ACME -Hh 10.1.3.30 -x 'net group "Domain Admins" /domain'

              [--dir-only] [--no-write-check] [-q] [--depth DEPTH]
              [--exclude SHARE [SHARE ...]] [-A PATTERN] [-F PATTERN]
              [--search-path PATH] [--search-timeout TIMEOUT]
              [--download PATH] [--upload SRC DST] [--delete PATH TO FILE]

                        ex '(web|global).(asax|config)'

File Content Search:
  Options for searching the content of files (must run as root), kind of experimental

  -F PATTERN            File content search, -F '[Pp]assword' (requires admin
                        access to execute commands, and PowerShell on victim
                        host)
  --search-path PATH    Specify drive/path to search (used with -F, default
                        C:\Users), ex 'D:\HR\'
  --search-timeout TIMEOUT
                        Specifcy a timeout (in seconds) before the file search
                        job gets killed. Default is 300 seconds.

Filesystem interaction:
  Options for interacting with the specified host's filesystem

  --download PATH       Download a file from the remote system,
                        ex.'C$\temp\passwords.txt'
  --upload SRC DST      Upload a file to the remote system ex.
                        '/tmp/payload.exe C$\temp\payload.exe'
  --delete PATH TO FILE
                        Delete a remote file, ex. 'C$\temp\msf.exe'
  --skip                Skip delete file confirmation prompt

Examples:

$ smbmap -u jsmith -p password1 -d workgroup -H 192.168.0.1
$ smbmap -u jsmith -p 'aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee:da76f2c4c96028b7a6111aef4a50a94d' -H 172.16.0.20
$ smbmap -u 'apadmin' -p 'asdf1234!' -d ACME -Hh 10.1.3.30 -x 'net group "Domain Admins" /domain'

local_help.man:
SMBMAP(1)                        User Commands                        SMBMAP(1)

NAME
     smbmap - SMB enumeration tool

SYNOPSIS
     smbmap  [-h]  (-H  HOST  |  --host-file  FILE)  [-u USERNAME] [-p PASSWORD
     |--prompt] [-s SHARE] [-d DOMAIN] [-P PORT] [-v]  [--admin]  [--no-banner]
     [--no-color]  [--no-update]  [-x COMMAND][--mode CMDMODE] [-L | -r [PATH]]
     [-A PATTERN | -g FILE | --csv  FILE]  [--dir-only][--no-write-check]  [-q]
     [--depth  DEPTH] [--exclude SHARE [SHARE ...]] [-F PATTERN] [--search-path
     PATH] [--search-timeout TIMEOUT]  [--download  PATH]  [--upload  SRC  DST]
     [--delete PATH TO FILE] [--skip]

DESCRIPTION
     SMBMap  allows  users to enumerate samba share drives across an entire do-
     main. List share drives, drive permissions, share  contents,  upload/down-
     load functionality, file name auto-download pattern matching, and even ex-
     ecute  remote  commands.  This tool was designed with pen testing in mind,
     and is intended to  simplify  searching  for  potentially  sensitive  data
     across large networks.

OPTIONS
   Main arguments:
     -H HOST
            IP of host

     --host-file FILE
            File containing a list of hosts

     -u USERNAME, --username USERNAME
            Username, if omitted null session assumed

     -p PASSWORD, --password PASSWORD
            Password or NTLM hash

     --prompt
            Prompt for a password

     -s SHARE
            Specify a share (default C$), ex 'C$'

     -d DOMAIN
            Domain name (default WORKGROUP)

     -P PORT
            SMB port (default 445)

     -v, --version
            Return the OS version of the remote host

     --signing
            Check if host has SMB signing disabled, enabled, or required

     --admin
            Just report if the user is an admin

     --no-banner
            Removes the banner from the top of the output

     --no-color
            Removes the color from output

     --no-update
            Removes the "Working on it" message

     --timeout SCAN_TIMEOUT
            Set port scan socket timeout. Default is .5 seconds

   Kerberos settings:
     -k, --kerberos
            Use Kerberos authentication

     --no-pass
            Use CCache file (export KRB5CCNAME='~/current.ccache')

     --dc-ip IP or Host
            IP or FQDN of DC

   Command Execution:
            Options for executing commands on the specified host

     -x COMMAND
            Execute a command ex. 'ipconfig /all'

     --mode CMDMODE
            Set the execution method, wmi or psexec, default wmi

   Shard drive Search:
            Options  for  searching/enumerating the filesystem of the specified
            host

     -L     List all drives on the specified host, requires ADMIN rights.

     -r [PATH]
            Recursively    list     dirs     and     files     (no     shareath
            lists the root of ALL shares), ex. 'email/backup'

     -g FILE
            Output to a file in a grep friendly format, used with -r (otherwise
            it outputs nothing), ex -g grep_out.txt

     -A PATTERN
            Define  a file name pattern (regex) that auto downloads a file on a
            match     (requires     -r),     not     case     sensitive,     ex
            '(web|global).(asax|config)'

     --csv FILE
            Output to a CSV file, ex --csv shares.csv

     --dir-only
            List only directories, omit files

     --no-write-check
            Skip check to see if drive grants WRITE access

     -q     Quiet  verbose output. Only shows shares you have READ or WRITE on,
            and suppresses file listing when performing a search (-A).

     --depth DEPTH
            Traverse a directory tree to a specific depth. Default is  1  (root
            node).

     --exclude SHARE [SHARE ...]
            Exclude  share(s)  from searching and listing, ex. --exclude ADMIN$
            C$'

   File Content Search:
            Options for searching the content of files (must run as root), kind
            of experimental

     -F PATTERN
            File content search, -F '[Pp]assword' (requries admin access to ex-
            ecute commands, and powershell on victim host)

     --search-path PATH
            Specify drive/path to search (used with -F, default  C:\Users),  ex
            'D:\HR\'

     --search-timeout TIMEOUT
            Specifcy  a  timeout  (in  seconds) before the file search job gets
            killed. Default is 300 seconds

   Filesystem interaction:
            Options for interacting with the specified host's filesystem

     --download PATH
            Download a file from the remote system, ex.'C$\temp\passwords.txt'

     --upload SRC DST
            Upload  a  file  to  the  remote  system   ex.    '/tmp/payload.exe
            C$\temp\payload.exe'

     --delete PATH_TO_FILE
            Delete a remote file, ex. 'C$\temp\msf.exe'

     --skip
            Skip delete file confirmation prompt

EXAMPLES:
     $ python smbmap.py -u jsmith -p password1 -d workgroup -H 192.168.0.1
     $          python          smbmap.py          -u         jsmith         -p
     'aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee:da76f2c4c96028b7a6111aef4a50a94d'     -H
     172.16.0.20
      $  python  smbmap.py  -u 'apadmin' -p 'asdf1234!' -d ACME -H 10.1.3.30 -x
     'net group "Domain Admins" /domain'

AUTHOR
     smbmap was developed by ShawnDEvans <ShawnDEvans@gmail.com>

     This manual page was written by Samuel Henrique <samueloph@debian.org> for
     the Debian project, it was based on smbmap -h output and can  be  used  by
     other projects as well.

smbmap 1.10.7                     January 2025                        SMBMAP(1)

apt_metadata.description:
handy SMB enumeration tool
SMBMap allows users to enumerate samba share drives across an entire domain.
List share drives, drive permissions, share contents, upload/download
functionality, file name auto-download pattern matching, and even execute
remote commands. This tool was designed with pen testing in mind, and is
intended to simplify searching for potentially sensitive data across large
networks.
Features:
Pass-the-Hash Support
File upload/download/delete
Permission enumeration (writable share, meet Metasploit)
Remote Command Execution
Distrubted file content searching (beta!)
File name matching (with an auto downoad capability)
Host file parser supports IPs, host names, and CIDR
SMB sigining detection
Server version output
Kerberos support! (super beta)

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use smbmap because smbmap - SMB enumeration tool.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `smbmap  [-h] (-H HOST | --host-file FILE) [-u USERNAME] [-p PASSWORD=$KFIOSA_SECRET [-s SHARE] [-d` as documented upstream for smbmap.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair smbmap with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `smbmap --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
command_examples: smbmap  [-h] (-H HOST | --host-file FILE) [-u USERNAME] [-p PASSWORD=$KFIOSA_SECRET [-s SHARE] [-d

arguments.name: -A
arguments.description: Define a file name pattern (regex) that auto downloads a file on a match (requires -r),

arguments.name: --admin
arguments.description: Just report if the user is an admin

arguments.name: --csv
arguments.description: Output to a CSV file, ex --csv shares.csv

arguments.name: -d
arguments.description: Domain name (default WORKGROUP)

arguments.name: --delete
arguments.description: Delete a remote file, ex. 'C$\temp\msf.exe'

arguments.name: --depth
arguments.description: Traverse a directory tree to a specific depth. Default is 1 (root node).

arguments.name: --dir-only
arguments.description: List only directories, omit files

arguments.name: --download
arguments.description: Download a file from the remote system, ex.'C$\temp\passwords.txt'

arguments.name: -g
arguments.description: Output to a file in a grep friendly format, used with -r (otherwise it outputs noth-

arguments.name: --host-file
arguments.description: File containing a list of hosts

arguments.name: -k
arguments.description: Use Kerberos authentication

arguments.name: --kerberos
arguments.description: Use Kerberos authentication

arguments.name: -L
arguments.description: List all drives on the specified host, requires ADMIN rights.

arguments.name: --mode
arguments.description: Set the execution method, wmi or psexec, default wmi

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Removes the banner from the top of the output

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Removes the color from output

arguments.name: --no-pass
arguments.description: Use CCache file (export KRB5CCNAME='~/current.ccache')

arguments.name: --no-update
arguments.description: Removes the "Working on it" message

arguments.name: --no-write-check
arguments.description: Skip check to see if drive grants WRITE access

arguments.name: -P
arguments.description: SMB port (default 445)

arguments.name: --prompt
arguments.description: Prompt for a password

arguments.name: -q
arguments.description: Quiet verbose output. Only shows shares you have READ or WRITE on, and suppresses file

arguments.name: -r
arguments.description: Recursively list dirs and files (no shareath

arguments.name: -s
arguments.description: Specify a share (default C$), ex 'C$'

arguments.name: --search-path
arguments.description: Specify drive/path to search (used with -F, default C:\Users), ex 'D:\HR\'

arguments.name: --search-timeout
arguments.description: Specifcy a timeout (in seconds) before the file search job gets killed. Default is 300

arguments.name: --signing
arguments.description: Check if host has SMB signing disabled, enabled, or required

arguments.name: --skip
arguments.description: Skip delete file confirmation prompt

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Set port scan socket timeout. Default is .5 seconds

arguments.name: -v
arguments.description: Return the OS version of the remote host

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Return the OS version of the remote host

arguments.name: -x
arguments.description: Execute a command ex. 'ipconfig /all'

examples: smbmap  [-h] (-H HOST | --host-file FILE) [-u USERNAME] [-p PASSWORD=$KFIOSA_SECRET [-s SHARE] [-d

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: smbmap - SMB enumeration tool.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: smbmap - SMB enumeration tool.
